mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings deterioration, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, invalidating the negative predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate revenues and equity prices over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52 percent, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth during periods of elevated inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate risk-off selling and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO accelerates alongside CPIAUCSL above 3.0 percent, contradicting the tightening-fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI increase exceeding 3.5 percent erodes real corporate margins and prompts market pricing of restrictive monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in over 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5 percent threshold, demonstrating a breakdown in the inflation-aversion channel.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive equity market returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes drops below 0.01 over a 24-month rolling window, or positive payroll surprises consistently coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling real GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumer leverage, which inevitably leads to demand collapse and negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing, or accuracy remains below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces demand destruction, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative in more than 55 percent of instances where INDPRO rises while FEDFUNDS increases, invalidating the supply-resilience premium.
